message:
🌱 Add envoy: Support for X-Forwarded-Host header mission (#1711)

Signed-off-by: github-actions[bot] <41898282+github-actions[bot]@users.noreply.github.com>
Co-authored-by: github-actions[bot] <41898282+github-actions[bot]@users.noreply.github.com>

files.patch:
@@ -0,0 +1,74 @@
+{
+  "version": "kc-mission-v1",
+  "name": "envoy-5940-support-for-x-forwarded-host-header",
+  "missionClass": "solution",
+  "author": "KubeStellar Bot",
+  "authorGithub": "kubestellar",
+  "mission": {
+    "title": "envoy: Support for X-Forwarded-Host header",
+    "description": "Support for X-Forwarded-Host header. Requested by 13+ users.",
+    "type": "feature",
+    "status": "completed",
+    "steps": [
+      {
+        "title": "Check current envoy deployment",
+        "description": "Verify your envoy version and configuration:\n```bash\nkubectl get pods -n envoy -l app.kubernetes.io/name=envoy\nhelm list -n envoy 2>/dev/null || echo \"Not installed via Helm\"\n```\nThis feature requires a working envoy installation."
+      },
+      {
+        "title": "Review envoy configuration",
+        "description": "Inspect the relevant envoy configuration:\n```bash\nkubectl get all -n envoy -l app.kubernetes.io/name=envoy\nkubectl get configmap -n envoy -l app.kubernetes.io/part-of=envoy\n```\n*Title*: *Support for X-Forwarded-Host header*\n\n*Description*:\nToday envoy does not seem to forward the original host in a X-Forwarded-Host header, it just rewrites the original one and apparently there is no way to get the original host back again."
+      },
+      {
+        "title": "Apply the fix for Support for X-Forwarded-Host header",
+        "description": "SGTM. I'm not sure if we want to config guard it or not. On one hand, I think it would be OK to not config guard if we only set when a host mutation happens as you point out. On the other hand, it's possible that upstreams are not expecting us to set it and we might break them. /maintainers any\n```yaml\n+----------+\n |          |\n |   App    |\n |          |\n +----------+\n      ^ Host: app.namespace.svc.cluster.local\n      | X-Forwarded-Host: example.com\n +----------+\n |          |\n |  Envoy   |\n |          |\n +----------+\n      ^\n      | Host:example.com\n      |\n      |\n     User\n```"
+      },
+      {
+        "title": "Verify the feature works",
+        "description": "Test that the new capability is working as expected:\n```bash\nkubectl get pods -n envoy -l app.kubernetes.io/name=envoy\nkubectl get events -n envoy --sort-by='.lastTimestamp' | tail -10\n```\nConfirm the feature described in \"Support for X-Forwarded-Host header\" is functioning correctly."
+      }
+    ],
+    "resolution": {
+      "summary": "SGTM. I'm not sure if we want to config guard it or not. On one hand, I think it would be OK to not config guard if we only set when a host mutation happens as you point out. On the other hand, it's possible that upstreams are not expecting us to set it and we might break them. /maintainers any thoughts?",
+      "codeSnippets": [
+        "+----------+\n |          |\n |   App    |\n |          |\n +----------+\n      ^ Host: app.namespace.svc.cluster.local\n      | X-Forwarded-Host: example.com\n +----------+\n |          |\n |  Envoy   |\n |          |\n +----------+\n      ^\n      | Host:example.com\n      |\n      |\n     User"
+      ]
+    }
+  },
+  "metadata": {
+    "tags": [
+      "envoy",
+      "graduated",
+      "networking",
+      "feature"
+    ],
+    "cncfProjects": [
+      "envoy"
+    ],
+    "targetResourceKinds": [],
+    "difficulty": "intermediate",
+    "issueTypes": [
+      "feature"
+    ],
+    "maturity": "graduated",
+    "sourceUrls": {
+      "issue": "https://github.com/envoyproxy/envoy/issues/5940",
+      "repo": "https://github.com/envoyproxy/envoy"
+    },
+    "reactions": 13,
+    "comments": 11,
+    "synthesizedBy": "copilot"
+  },
+  "prerequisites": {
+    "kubernetes": ">=1.24",
+    "tools": [
+      "kubectl"
+    ],
+    "description": "A running Kubernetes cluster with envoy installed or the issue environment reproducible."
+  },
+  "security": {

+}
